daily_brief: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo continues to dominate global health concerns, with the WHO reporting a 30-50% mortality rate among confirmed cases and over 1,000 suspected infections two weeks after declaration [BBC, Guardian, RFI]. MSF has warned this is the fastest-spreading Ebola outbreak on record, while WHO Director-General Tedros visited Ituri province to oversee containment efforts complicated by ongoing conflict and Western aid cuts. In European security developments, a Russian drone struck a residential building in the Romanian city of Galati, injuring two civilians and raising concerns about NATO's air defense capabilities along Ukraine's border [BBC]. At the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, Japan's Defence Minister Koizumi firmly rejected Chinese accusations of 'new militarism,' pointing to China's own nuclear arsenal and military expansion [BBC, Financial Times, The Hindu]. Israel announced its deepest incursion into Lebanon in over 25 years, capturing the strategic Beaufort Castle near Nabatiyeh as fighting with Hezbollah intensifies [France 24, The Hindu]. In Paris, over 400 people were detained following violent celebrations of PSG's Champions League victory over Arsenal, with 22,000 police deployed across France after similar unrest last year [BBC, RFI, France 24, Al Jazeera].

top_stories.headline: Ebola Outbreak in DRC Becomes Fastest-Spreading on Record as WHO Chief Visits Epicenter
bullets: WHO reports 30-50% death rate among confirmed Ebola cases, with over 1,000 suspected cases and at least 246 deaths two weeks after outbreak declaration
MSF warns this is the fastest-spreading Ebola outbreak ever recorded, with the response 'not yet caught up to the rapid spread'
WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus visited Bunia in Ituri province to inaugurate a treatment center and meet with officials
Trump administration building quarantine facility in Kenya for Americans rather than allowing them to return to the US; green card holders from DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan banned from entering US

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized impact of Western aid cuts on outbreak response capacity

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on MSF warnings and scale of outbreak

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Highlighted WHO chief's visit and community engagement efforts

top_stories.headline: Russian Drone Strikes Residential Building in Romanian NATO Member State
bullets: Attack drone hit apartment building in Galati, Romania early Friday morning while residents slept, punching hole in roof
Woman and teenage son hospitalized with bruises and minor burns; building damage limited because drone struck lift shaft
Residents express frustration that Romania's air force could not intercept the drone despite advance warning alerts
Incident highlights growing danger to NATO countries bordering Ukraine from Russian drone attacks

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on resident experiences and NATO protection concerns

top_stories.headline: Japan Rejects China's 'New Militarism' Accusations at Singapore Security Summit
bullets: Japanese Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi rejected Beijing's accusations of 'new militarism' at Shangri-La Dialogue
Koizumi pointed to China's nuclear arsenal and military expansion as 'serious concern' to international community
Japan has set defense spending records for 12 consecutive years, with latest budget exceeding $57 billion
China's defense ministry had warned days earlier that 'the grey rhino of a remilitarised Japan is gathering speed'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided historical context of WWII tensions

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Japan's pivot under PM Takaichi and Taiwan intervention comments

top_stories.headline: Israel Captures Beaufort Castle in Deepest Lebanon Incursion in 26 Years
bullets: Israeli forces captured strategic medieval Beaufort Castle near Nabatiyeh in southern Lebanon after days of intense fighting
Capture represents Israel's deepest ground incursion into Lebanon since withdrawal in 2000
Israeli military announced forces are 'expanding to additional areas' in Lebanon; one soldier killed by Hezbollah drone
25 Israelis killed since hostilities resumed March 2 when Hezbollah reopened front following Israeli-US strikes on Iran

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided liveblog format with multiple updates

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Noted direct talks between Israel and Lebanon occurring in Washington simultaneously

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Hezbollah rocket attacks on northern Israel beaches

top_stories.headline: Over 400 Arrested Across France Following Violent PSG Champions League Celebrations
bullets: 416 people detained nationwide including 283 in Paris after PSG defeated Arsenal on penalties to retain Champions League title
22,000 police deployed across France including 8,000 in Paris; tram lines halted and metro stations closed preventatively
Seven police officers injured; six vehicles, two businesses, and bus shelter damaged; fans stormed Paris ring road with flares
Interior Minister Nunez called unrest 'absolutely unacceptable'; authorities better prepared than last year when celebrations turned deadly

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Noted last year's celebrations turned deadly

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed police deployment numbers and preventive measures

additional_stories.headline: Death toll from Washington state chemical plant implosion rises to 11 as all missing workers recovered
San Antonio Spurs defeat Oklahoma City Thunder to reach NBA Finals, with Victor Wembanyama scoring 22 points
Australia to receive only used nuclear submarines from US under amended AUKUS deal, defense minister calls it 'cost-effective'
Trump claims Iran has agreed to no nuclear weapons development amid reports he sent back 'tougher' peace proposal
Italy bans Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns following Jewish community request
US demands 50% American content requirement for North American autos in USMCA renegotiation talks with Mexico
Dormitory fire at Kenyan girls' school kills at least 16 students; survivors report student lit mattress with match
Ardern documentary 'Prime Minister' wins top prize at 2026 News & Documentary Emmy Awards
Trump to headline America's 250th anniversary celebration after musical artists drop out over event's ties to president
